findings.reason: เผื่อ (pheua) has a high-class initial consonant (ผ) with a mai ek tone mark, which produces a low tone, not falling.

findings.reason: โปร่ง (bproeng) has a mid-class initial consonant cluster (ปร) with a mai ek tone mark, which produces a low tone, not mid.

findings.thai: ฝนตกตั้งแต่เช้า
findings.reason: เช้า (chao) has a low-class initial consonant (ช) with a mai tho tone mark, which produces a high tone, not falling.

findings.thai: ฝนตกหนักเชียว
findings.reason: หนัก (nak) has a high-class leading consonant (ห) making the syllable follow high-class rules. With a short vowel and a dead ending (ก), it produces a low tone, not high.

findings.thai: ฝากกุญแจไว้
findings.reason: ฝาก (faak) has a high-class initial consonant (ฝ) with a long vowel and a dead ending (ก), which produces a low tone, not falling.

findings.thai: พยายามแก้เองทั้งที่ไม่รู้วิธี
findings.romanization: pha-yaa-yaam gaae eeng thang-thii mai ruu wi-thii
findings.reason: The current tones array is missing one tone (10 tones for 11 syllables). พ (pha) is high tone, ทั้ง (thang) is high tone, ไม่ (mai) is falling tone, วิ (wi) is high tone.
พรุ่ง (phrung) has a low-class initial consonant cluster (พร) with a mai ek tone mark, which produces a falling tone, not mid.

findings.thai: พรุ่งนี้จะยุ่ง
findings.reason: พรุ่ง (phrung) has a low-class initial consonant cluster (พร) with a mai ek tone mark, which produces a falling tone, not high.
ก็ (gaw) is an exception word that is pronounced with a falling tone, not low.
พอ (phaw) has a low-class initial consonant (พ) with a long vowel and a live ending, which produces a mid tone, not falling.
